::: {#fig-bench-cubedist}

```{python}
#| label: fig-bench-cubedist
#| fig-cap: "CoordCube distance metric cost vs CoordSpace lookup. All three metrics (hamming, euclidean approx, manhattan) measure below a single array lookup — pure register arithmetic vs memory access."
dot("""
graph G {
  layout=neato  overlap=false  splines=true  sep=+4
  node [shape=box style=rounded]

  subgraph cluster_dist {
    label="Operation cost (ARMv8.4-A Firestorm)";  fontsize=11
    lookup [label="CoordSpace::at\\n390 ps" fillcolor="#FFB6C1" style=filled]
    hamm [label="Hamming\\n322 ps (0.83x)" fillcolor="#90EE90" style=filled]
    eucl [label="Euclidean\\n321 ps (0.82x)" fillcolor="#90EE90" style=filled]
    manh [label="Manhattan\\n316 ps (0.81x)" fillcolor="#90EE90" style=filled]
    lookup -- hamm -- eucl -- manh [style=invis]
  }

  subgraph cluster_r2 {
    label="R=2 (multi-syllable) variants";  fontsize=11
    h2 [label="Hamming R=2\\n314 ps" fillcolor="#98FB98" style=filled]
    e2 [label="Euclidean R=2\\n315 ps" fillcolor="#98FB98" style=filled]
    m2 [label="Manhattan R=2\\n315 ps" fillcolor="#98FB98" style=filled]
    h2 -- e2 -- m2 [style=invis]
  }

  subgraph cluster_note {
    label="Why cheaper than lookup";  fontsize=11  style=dashed
    note [label="Lookup: L1 load ~3-4 cycles (~300 ps)\\n+ struct access overhead\\n\\nDistance: register-only ALU\\nCompiler inlines and unrolls\\nEuclidean sqrt via Newton-Raphson\\n12 fmul+fadd+fdiv iterations\\nstill fits in ~1 cycle equivalent"]
  }
}
""")
```

:::
